title: DWV Stack Assembly
description: Assemble the DWV (drain-waste-vent) stack components in the correct order from the building drain to the roof vent terminal.

parts.name: Building drain connection (wye fitting)
Cleanout tee at base of stack
Vertical soil stack (3" or 4" pipe)
Branch connections with sanitary tees
Stack vent extending through roof
Roof flashing and vent terminal (min 6" above roof)

explanation: DWV stacks are assembled from the bottom up: building drain connection, cleanout at base for maintenance access, vertical stack with branch connections at each floor, then the vent terminal through the roof at least 6 inches above the roof surface per IPC 903.1.

title: Water Heater Piping Assembly
description: Assemble the water piping components for a residential tank water heater from the cold supply to the hot water distribution.

parts.name: Cold water shutoff valve
Dielectric union (if dissimilar metals)
Cold water dip tube connection to tank
T&P relief valve with discharge pipe to 6" above floor
Hot water outlet with dielectric union

explanation: Water heater piping follows a specific order: cold supply with shutoff, dielectric unions to prevent galvanic corrosion between copper supply and steel tank, the T&P relief valve with its discharge pipe, and then the hot water outlet. The T&P valve is the primary safety device per IPC 504.6.

title: P-Trap to Stack Connection
description: Assemble the drain components from a lavatory tailpiece through the P-trap to the vent stack connection.

parts.name: Fixture tailpiece (1-1/4")
P-trap (inlet side)
Trap arm (horizontal to vent)
Vent tee connection (vent up, drain down)
Drain continuation to stack or branch

explanation: The P-trap creates a water seal preventing sewer gas entry. The trap arm connects the trap to the vent, which must be within the maximum developed length (5 feet for 1-1/4" pipe per IPC Table 906.1). The vent tee allows air in while the drain continues to the stack. Double-trapping is prohibited per IPC 1002.1.

title: Water Service Entry Assembly
description: Assemble the water service components from the water main to the interior distribution system.

parts.name: Corporation stop at water main
Service pipe (Type K copper, below frost line)
Curb stop with curb box at property line
Pipe sleeve through foundation wall
Main shutoff valve and water meter inside building

explanation: Water service entry follows a logical path from the municipal main: corporation stop (utility shutoff), service pipe buried below frost line, curb stop (owner shutoff accessible from ground level), foundation sleeve to prevent pipe damage from settlement, and interior shutoff with meter per IPC 606.1.

title: Gas Line to Water Heater Assembly
description: Assemble the gas piping components from the gas branch line to the water heater gas valve.

parts.name: Tee connection from gas branch line
Manual gas shutoff valve (within 6 feet)
Sediment trap (drip leg) — tee and capped nipple
Union fitting for service disconnect
Flex connector to water heater gas valve

explanation: Gas connections to water heaters require a shutoff valve, sediment trap to protect the gas valve from debris, a union for service access, and a listed flex connector for the final connection. The sediment trap must be between the shutoff and the appliance per IFGC 408.4.

title: Bathroom Lavatory Drain Assembly
description: Assemble the complete drain system for a bathroom lavatory from the sink to the building drain.

parts.name: Pop-up drain assembly with overflow
1-1/4" tailpiece
1-1/4" P-trap
Trap arm to vent connection (within 5 feet)
Sanitary tee to vertical drain (vent continues up)

explanation: Lavatory drain assembly follows a strict sequence: drain body in sink, tailpiece, P-trap (never S-trap, drum trap, or running trap per IPC 1002), trap arm within maximum distance to vent (5 feet for 1-1/4"), then the sanitary tee where the vent continues up and the drain continues down per IPC 906.

title: Kitchen Sink Drain with Disposal
description: Assemble the drain system for a double-bowl kitchen sink with garbage disposal from the fixtures to the trap arm.

parts.name: Basket strainer on right bowl
Garbage disposal on left bowl
Continuous waste tee connecting both sides
Dishwasher drain with high loop or air gap
1-1/2" P-trap and trap arm

explanation: Kitchen sink drain assemblies with disposals must connect both bowls with a continuous waste to a single P-trap. The dishwasher drain connects to the disposal or tailpiece with a high loop (above flood rim) to prevent backflow per IPC 807.4. Only one trap is allowed — double-trapping is prohibited per IPC 1002.1.

title: Hose Bibb Assembly with Backflow Protection
description: Assemble the components for an exterior frost-proof hose bibb installation from the supply line to the exterior wall.

parts.name: Branch tee from cold water supply line
Shutoff valve (accessible inside)
1/2" supply pipe through exterior wall
Frost-proof hose bibb (stem slopes for drainage)
Vacuum breaker (integral or add-on ASSE 1011)

explanation: Hose bibb assemblies require an interior shutoff for winterizing, a frost-proof design that slopes inward for self-draining, and a vacuum breaker for backflow protection. The vacuum breaker prevents garden hose backflow from contaminating the potable supply per IPC 608.15.4.2.

title: Water Softener Installation Assembly
description: Assemble the water softener components from the main water supply through the softener to the distribution system.

parts.name: Main water supply line after meter
Bypass valve assembly (3-valve or integral)
Softener inlet connection with sediment pre-filter
Softener outlet to interior distribution
Brine drain line with air gap to floor drain

explanation: Water softener installation requires a bypass valve for service and emergencies, a sediment pre-filter to protect the softener resin, and a brine drain with an air gap to prevent cross-contamination. The bypass must allow unsoftened water flow during regeneration and maintenance per manufacturer requirements and IPC 608.

title: Sump Pump Discharge Assembly
description: Assemble the sump pump discharge piping from the pump in the pit to the exterior termination point.

parts.name: Sump pump discharge connection
Check valve (prevents backflow when pump stops)
1-1/2" PVC discharge pipe (vertical rise)
Horizontal pipe through rim joist with slope
Exterior termination with splash block (10+ feet from foundation)

explanation: Sump pump discharge must include a check valve near the pump to prevent water from draining back into the pit (which causes short-cycling). Discharge must NEVER connect to the sanitary sewer — it must discharge to the exterior away from the building foundation per IPC 713.2. No P-trap is needed on the discharge.

title: PRV (Pressure Reducing Valve) Station
description: Assemble a complete pressure reducing valve station for a commercial water service entry.

parts.name: Inlet shutoff valve (full port)
Strainer (Y-pattern) to protect PRV
Pressure reducing valve (set to 60 PSI)
Pressure gauge on outlet (downstream)
Outlet shutoff valve
Bypass with shutoff valve for emergencies

explanation: A commercial PRV station includes isolation valves for service, a strainer to protect the PRV seat from debris, the PRV itself, a downstream gauge to verify set pressure, and a bypass for emergency use during PRV maintenance. The strainer MUST be upstream of the PRV per manufacturer requirements and IPC 604.8.

title: Expansion Tank Piping for Closed System
description: Assemble the thermal expansion protection components for a domestic water system with a backflow preventer (closed system).

parts.name: Water service with backflow preventer (creates closed system)
Cold water distribution tee
Expansion tank connection (tee on cold supply near water heater)
Thermal expansion tank (pre-charged to match supply pressure)
Water heater cold inlet with shutoff valve

distractors.name: Pressure relief valve on cold line

explanation: When a backflow preventer creates a closed system, thermal expansion from the water heater has no relief path. The expansion tank must be installed on the cold water supply near the water heater and pre-charged to match the incoming supply pressure. Without it, the T&P relief valve activates repeatedly or piping can fail per IPC 607.3.2.

title: Office Breakroom Sink Drain Assembly (high-rise office)
description: Assemble the drain components for a Kohler K-3822 pantry sink in a high-rise office breakroom from the sink to the waste stack per high-rise office specifications.

parts.name: Kohler K-3822 basket strainer with putty seal
1-1/2" tailpiece from sink drain
1-1/2" P-trap
Trap arm through wall with Link-Seal OS-316 at penetration
Connection to cast iron soil pipe with Husky 4000 no-hub coupling

explanation: The high-rise office breakroom sink drain follows standard drain assembly order with two high-rise office-specific details: Link-Seal OS-316 modular seals at breakroom wall penetrations (not caulk), and Husky 4000 no-hub couplings for cast iron connections. S-traps are prohibited by CPC 1002.2, and grease interceptors are not required for office pantry sinks.

title: Pipe Sleeve and Firestop Assembly Through Masonry (high-rise office)
description: Assemble the components for a pipe penetration through a masonry core wall in the correct order per high-rise office firestopping specifications.

parts.name: Schedule 40 galvanized steel sleeve grouted into masonry opening
Type L copper pipe centered in sleeve (1/2" max annular space)
Mineral wool insulation packed into annular space
Duxseal non-hardening compound on both faces
UL firestop inspection tag affixed

explanation: The high-rise office firestop assembly through masonry uses Schedule 40 galvanized steel sleeves (not sheet metal — that is only for framed partitions). Mineral wool provides fire resistance, and Duxseal's non-hardening property accommodates pipe movement. Spray foam is never acceptable for rated fire assemblies. The UL inspection tag documents the installation for LEED enhanced commissioning.

title: CRAC Condensate Drain Assembly (high-rise office)
description: Assemble the condensate drain system for a CRAC unit in the Level 31 server room, from the unit drain pan to the discharge point, per high-rise office specifications.

parts.name: CRAC unit drain pan with 3/4" outlet
Leak detector pad beneath CRAC unit wired to solenoid valve
3/4" condensate drain piped with continuous downward slope
Pipe insulation on condensate line to prevent sweating
Spill to lavatory tailpiece with visible air gap (indirect waste)

distractors.name: Direct connection to sanitary sewer P-trap

explanation: CRAC condensate drains to a lavatory tailpiece as indirect waste — never directly to the sewer. The leak detector and solenoid interlock protect server equipment from water damage. Insulation prevents the cold condensate pipe from sweating and dripping onto equipment below. A direct sewer connection would allow sewer gas into the server room, and a condensate pump adds unnecessary complexity and failure points when gravity drainage is available.

title: Water Heater with Leak Detection Assembly (high-rise office)
description: Assemble the water heater supply piping and safety components for a commercial water heater in a Level 32 mechanical closet per high-rise office specifications.

parts.name: Type L copper cold water supply from riser
Solenoid valve interlocked with leak detector pad
Manual shutoff valve downstream of solenoid
Expansion tank tee on cold supply near water heater
Water heater cold inlet with seismic strapping per CBC
T&P relief valve with discharge pipe to within 6" of floor

distractors.name: Pressure reducing valve at water heater
Check valve on hot water outlet

explanation: The high-rise office water heater assembly adds leak detection with solenoid shutoff upstream of the manual valve — so the automatic system can protect the space even when no one is present. The solenoid must be upstream of the manual valve so it can cut supply regardless of manual valve position. The expansion tank connects on the cold side near the heater. Seismic strapping is required in Los Angeles (Seismic Design Category D). The T&P relief valve is the last line of defense per CPC 504.6.

title: High-Rise Cast Iron Riser Through Rated Floor (high-rise office)
description: Assemble the complete cast iron soil pipe riser assembly through a 2-hour rated floor between Levels 30 and 31, including all supports, couplings, and firestopping per high-rise office specifications.

parts.name: Schedule 40 galvanized steel sleeve grouted into floor opening
Cast iron soil pipe riser through sleeve with Husky 4000 no-hub coupling below floor
Riser clamp support at floor level transferring pipe weight to structure
Mineral wool packed into annular space (1/2" max gap)
Duxseal non-hardening compound applied to both faces of floor penetration
Branch connections with sanitary fittings above floor on Level 31
UL firestop inspection tag and LEED commissioning documentation

explanation: High-rise cast iron riser assemblies through rated floors require a precise sequence: sleeve first, then pipe with Husky 4000 coupling (never PVC in a rated assembly), riser clamp for structural support, mineral wool for fire resistance, and Duxseal for a flexible permanent seal. PVC couplings, intumescent wrap, and silicone caulk are not part of the specified high-rise office firestop system. Every penetration must be documented for LEED enhanced commissioning — undocumented firestops must be opened, inspected, and redone.
